steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content:
The passage opens by naming what Smith is “famed for,” then immediately deflates it with “putatively.” Smith deployed the invisible hand metaphor only once, it was ignored, and twentieth-century economists elevated it to promote their own views.

The passage explains this reputational inflation, not the metaphor's actual merit.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
C directly identifies that Smith's reputation came from later promotion, not from the metaphor's importance in his work.
  • A wrongly adds an endorsement of the metaphor
  • B misfocuses on Smith's intention rather than later promotion
  • D misframes the elevation as discovery rather than strategic motivation
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
